template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Top-down silhouette of a 75% board. The lapis splash sits as a contiguous block across the left half of the alpha cluster (Q W E R on the top alpha row and A S D F on the home row), reading as the glazed-brickwork lapis of the Ishtar Gate. The spacebar is tinted sandstone — a warm cream/tan — as the secondary nod to the cream/sandstone reliefs of lions and aurochs on the original Babylonian gate. Warm-bronze hairlines on the top and bottom case edges only. No literal gate or animal motifs — silhouette + palette tells the story.
brief_summary: Hero art for the news-pillar companion piece to the GMK CYL Ishtar R2 group-buy listing. R2 is the second round of the same Ishtar set; the palette is unchanged from the original — deep lapis blue with cream/sandstone reliefs. The composition mirrors the in-house GMK CYL hero language (top-down 75% silhouette, warm-bronze hairlines, single splash of color on the alpha cluster) but substitutes lapis for coral and adds a sandstone-tinted spacebar as a secondary accent — the only deviation from the strict 'single splash' rule, justified by the original set itself being a two-color story (lapis + cream) where the cream is structurally distinct from the lapis. Reads at a glance as 'a GMK CYL companion piece, but lapis-and-sandstone instead of coral.'
composition_decision: single contiguous lapis block across QWER + ASDF (eight caps) as the primary splash, plus a sandstone-tinted spacebar as a quiet secondary accent for the cream-relief half of the Ishtar palette; both accents are abstract — no gate, no aurochs, no lions

splash_color.name: lapis
splash_color.rationale: slightly-greenish-deep-blue keyed to lapis lazuli, not navy; matches the glazed-brickwork hue of the Ishtar Gate

secondary_accent.name: sandstone
secondary_accent.rationale: warm cream/tan nodding to the cream/sandstone reliefs of lions and aurochs on the original Ishtar Gate; isolated to the spacebar so it reads as a structural counterpoint to the lapis alpha block, not a second equal splash

bronze_accent.name: warm-bronze
bronze_accent.usage: 1.5px hairlines on the top and bottom case edges only, 0.65 stroke opacity

rationale: GMK CYL Ishtar draws from the Ishtar Gate of Babylon — glazed lapis brickwork with cream sandstone reliefs. R2 reissues the same palette, so the hero treats Ishtar as a lapis-and-sandstone signature: lapis splash on a contiguous alpha block (Q W E R + A S D F) for the brickwork, sandstone-tinted spacebar for the cream reliefs. Both accents stay abstract; the composition language otherwise matches the King-of-the-Seas and GREG 2 companion pieces so the GMK CYL group-buy companion family reads as a coherent set differentiated only by colorway.
tokens_snapshot: design/tokens.css — --kh-accent oklch(0.80 0.135 75); lapis and sandstone are bespoke to this hero, keyed to the original Ishtar set
layout_choice: 75% — single committed layout, identical row geometry to the GREG 2 hero (case y=200, rows at y=232/282/346/410/474/538). 1020x410 case rectangle, six rows.
style_guide: bearings.md § Article hero art (locked 2026-05-09): 2px warm-grey stroke throughout; primary splash on accent caps + warm-bronze accent on case edges only; transparent canvas. Ishtar-specific deviation: a single secondary sandstone tint on the spacebar to honor the two-color story of the source set without breaking the structural language.
xml_validity: validated — no double-hyphen runs inside comments; parses as well-formed XML
